author: Sayad Ali Bilgrami, Dr., Shams-ul-Ulama
title: Arbon ka Fan-i-Ta'mir
gloss: A booklet on the architecture of the Arabs with an account of the important buildings constructed by them in India, Persia, Syria, Baghdad, Egypt, Spain, etc.
notes: Date given only as July 1910. Islamic architectural history for popular readership; author bears the Shams-ul-Ulama title.

author: Shibli Numani, Maulana
title: Islami Hukumat aur Hindustan men uska Tamadani asar
gloss: A small pamphlet in which the writer describes what effect the Islamic régimé in India has had on the social and moral condition of the country.
notes: Date given only as June 1910. Shibli's civilizational-impact essay — Muslim historiographical self-defense in the colonial public sphere.

author: Shibli, Maulaua
title: Jahangir aur Tozak-i-Jahangiri
gloss: An essay in which the author tries to whitewash character of the Emperor Jahangir by means of quotations from Tazak-i-Jahangiri.
notes: Catalogue's gloss editorializes ('tries to whitewash') — the colonial cataloguer passing judgment on Shibli's Mughal historiography. Catalogue prints 'Anrssay' (misprint) and 'Maulaua'.

author: Shiv Brat Lal, Babu
title: Majun-i-Khayal
gloss: A booklet containing nine papers on spiritual and moral subjects, re-printed from the "Sadhu" Magazine of Lahore.
notes: Same author/publisher network as Hamari Mataen (p27 s39) — Sadhu Magazine reprints.

author: Waris-ud-din, Mr.
title: Dua ke Zariye Bimaron ke achhe hone ki Tisri Kitab
gloss: Third book of a series in which the author has collected the result of cases of patients cured merely by his prayers to God for their restoration to health.
notes: Faith-healing case reports from Clarkabad (Christian settlement village) — serialized (third book).

author: Fayyaz-ur-Rahman, Maulvi
title: Istilahat-i-Jugrafiya
publisher: Author, who is a teacher in the A.-V. Municipal Board School, Akyab
notes: Entry preceded by '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Author teaching in Akyab (Arakan, Burma), printing in Lahore — trans-provincial Urdu schoolbook circuit.
